note: The audited, replay-grade step record. Nothing hard-coded: the viewer reads this to know which fields each kind carries and how to label them. There are TEN step kinds (see step_kinds). A step is a TIME SPAN by one ACTOR; tool_calls separate input · output · analysis; gates + decisions carry the full option menu so you can replay what the human chose among. Steps may NEST via substeps[] — a parent step's inner steps form a TREE — and because every step is a time span the record reads as a TIMELINE: who did what, in what order, over what interval.

common_fields.id: stable step id — <phaseId>#<seq> — so decisions/evidence can reference a step
common_fields.seq: monotonic order within the phase (read top-to-bottom, no sort)
common_fields.kind: the discriminator — selects which fields are present
common_fields.actor: { id, role } — WHO acted (agent | a named operator | system). On EVERY step.
common_fields.headline: one scannable line (<=80 chars) — what happened, bold at the top of the step
common_fields.duration_ms: ended-started, rendered as a muted chip (· 4.2s); for a gate = dwell time, for a human = think time
common_fields.status: ok | error | denied | timeout | cached | skipped | raised | answered | expired | committed | pending (a suggestion awaiting its gate)
common_fields.substeps: OPTIONAL [step, …] — nested child steps that ran AS PART OF this step (a plan that fans out into its executions, a delegated/parallel sub-investigation, a tool that did sub-operations). Same step shape, recursively; the viewer renders them as an indented branch on the timeline. Top-level seq stays gap-free 1..n; substep seq is local to its parent (e.g. id <phaseId>#3.1).

plan.what: The ordered intents the agent commits to at phase start (promoted out of reasoning).

reasoning.what: The agent thinking — tagged by subtype so a plan reads differently from a conclusion.

fields.confidence: 0..1 — present on an analysis that forms a conclusion

tool_call.what: One capability invocation — the request, the raw return, and the agent's read of it, kept distinct.

fields.intent: the playbook need being satisfied (metrics(storage), traces, changes…)
fields.capability: the RESOLVED capability id actually invoked (provider/action)
fields.tool: { id, action, target } — human label of what was invoked
fields.input: the REAL args/query sent (the thing you replay)
fields.output: the REAL structured data returned — may carry candidates[] for multi-candidate returns
fields.analysis: the agent's interpretation of the output (NOT the data)
fields.access: allow | ask | deny — the govern() verdict at call time
fields.evidence: [{ ref, caption, kind }] — captioned, so the screenshot says what it proves
fields.touched: [node/edge ids this step read or wrote]
fields.graph_ops: [{ op, node|edge, to? }] — the graph mutation this step caused (the link step→graph)
fields.error: { type, message, transient } — on a failed/timed-out call
fields.retry_of: id of the step this retries
fields.idempotency_key: exactly-once key for a WRITE — a replay returns the cached result, never double-applies

observation.what: A finding folded from data WITHOUT a tool call (a correlation, a ruled-out conclusion) — an AUDITED graph annotate, distinct from free reasoning.

suggestion.what: The agent proposes an action and presents the full option menu.

fields.options: [{ id, label, kind, recommended, blast_radius?, rollback?, expected_effect?, risk? }] — the menu the operator chose among
fields.supersedes: id of the suggestion this replaces (after a deny/refine)
fields.diff: { from, to } — what changed vs the superseded proposal

gate.what: The pause itself — a first-class, replayable checkpoint, distinct from the decision that resolves it.

fields.gate: { kind: advance | write | close | escalation, phase, target_phase? }
fields.prompt: what the operator is being asked

decision.what: The operator's verdict at a gate — the accountability-critical event, self-contained.

fields.refinement: { keep?, change?, add_constraint? } — on refine, the delta the operator fed back
fields.reason: why the operator chose as they did
fields.in_response_to: the gate/suggestion step id this answers

user_input.what: Exactly what the operator typed into the shared chat.

fields.in_reply_to: seq/id of the step being responded to

escalation.what: Hand-off to a human/another team when the engine can't safely proceed (error_handler trip or a risk call).

backtrack.what: Re-opening a previously ruled-out node/conclusion when new evidence contradicts it.

graph_vocabulary.note: The signals a learning/automation layer keys on — enumerated here so they're schema-described, not reverse-engineered. (Authoritative node/edge/health lists live in graph-schema.json.)
graph_vocabulary.confirm_cause: On recovery, verify-close writes confirm_cause on the suspected_cause edge (→ confirmed) — the explicit action→outcome link that turns a closed incident into a reusable runbook.
